hypothesis: Discouraging visible-slot placeholders in required, non-enum, non-content string identifiers (e.g. Input.name) will stop the evaluator-flagged Input.name/Input.placeholder role duplication on the Auth OOD record without regressing other continuous quality metrics.

checkpoint.note: E620/E631's local-only checkpoint (sha256 3ce5c9ef...) was not present in this session's sandbox, so this iteration retrained the identical E620 800-step scratch recipe (same train dir, model, context backend, output tokenizer, device, steps, batch size, seed) to reproduce it. The retrained checkpoint's control-arm predictions are byte-identical to E631's treatment-arm predictions on this OOD n=4 suite, confirming the replay is faithful.

per_record_baseline.ood_gallery_01:
root = Stack([v0], "column")
v0 = ImageGallery([{src: ":ood.gallery.img", alt: ":ood.gallery.alt", details: ":ood.gallery.caption"}])
per_record_baseline.ood_auth_01:
root = Stack([v0, v1, v4], "column")
v0 = Button(":ood.auth.create")
v1 = Input(":ood.auth.email", ":ood.auth.name")
v4 = Input(":ood.auth.name", ":ood.auth.email")

per_record_treatment.ood_gallery_01:
root = Stack([v0], "column")
v0 = ImageGallery([{src: ":ood.gallery.img", alt: ":ood.gallery.alt", details: ":ood.gallery.caption"}])

weight_threshold_probe.description: Additional single-record replays (Auth only affected) at intermediate schema_identifier_decode_weight values, holding every other flag fixed, to check for a usable middle ground between no-effect and destructive.
weight_threshold_probe.0.5: byte-identical to the 4.0 treatment (destructive: Input wrapper disappears, raw slot tokens become bare Stack array items, plus a stray empty array)
weight_threshold_probe.conclusion: The bias has a knife-edge on/off threshold between weight 0.25 and 0.5 on this checkpoint; there is no intermediate magnitude in this session's probes that fixes the Input.name/Input.placeholder duplication without destabilizing the Auth repeated-array closure.

intervention_summary.mechanism: schema_identifier_decode_weight only fires inside a 'component' frame at an arg_index whose schema is a required, non-enum, plain 'string' type not tagged x-openui-placeholder (verified in isolation by a new unit test, test_schema_identifier_bias_penalizes_slots_only_for_non_content_identifiers, which is scoped exactly this way). On the Auth record it never lets a visible-slot value land in Input.name.
intervention_summary.observed_effect: Because this is a parallel MaskGIT-style denoiser (not autoregressive), discouraging the model's only confident continuation at Input.name propagates through remask/confidence ordering to upstream choices: slot_coverage_close_applications drops 25->22 and choice_changes 12->9, semantic_plan_root_applications drops 16->7, and the model abandons the Input(...) wrapper for the Auth record entirely -- emitting bare slot references as direct Stack array items (plus a stray empty array) instead of the two Input siblings. Dashboard, Gallery, and Modal predictions are unaffected (byte-identical to baseline) because their component/property structure never traverses schema_identifier_decode_weight's guarded arg positions.
intervention_summary.remaining_strict_failure: Strict meaning v2 was already 0.0 in the baseline and stays 0.0 in the treatment; this iteration regresses every other continuous metric instead of fixing the Auth role-duplication.

decision: Reject schema_identifier_decode_weight as a decode-time treatment on this checkpoint at every magnitude tested (0.25 no-op, 0.5/1.0/4.0 destructive). Keep the code as a default-off (weight=0.0) knob in the schema_value_bias/schema_opaque_bias family -- it is unit-tested in isolation, does not change any existing recipe's behavior when left at 0.0, and remains available for a future, better-trained checkpoint or a decode-order-aware follow-up. Do not enable it in any committed eval recipe. Do not sync, promote, or make a ship claim.
